description: Mutating UIKit/SwiftUI state from a background task without hopping to the main actor, causing crashes or undefined UI behavior.
body: Failure mode: a network or background task updates UI state directly off the main thread; UIKit/SwiftUI require main-thread/MainActor updates, so this triggers assertions, glitches, or crashes. Detection signals: Main Thread Checker / purple runtime warnings 'UI API called on a background thread'; intermittent UI corruption; crashes after async work completes. Do not update UI state off the main actor. Safer fallback: annotate the UI-owning type or method @MainActor, or hop with await MainActor.run { ... }, ensure async completions resume on the main actor, and verify the Main Thread Checker stays silent.
